* Fix logout: capture id_token before cookie sign-out

The /logout endpoint was signing out of the cookie scheme first, which
destroyed the authentication ticket containing the saved tokens. When
the OIDC sign-out then ran, it could not find the id_token to send as
id_token_hint to Keycloak, causing a 'Missing parameters: id_token_hint'
error page.

Fix: capture the id_token via GetTokenAsync before any sign-out, then
pass it to the OIDC sign-out via both AuthenticationProperties.Items
and StoreTokens so the OIDC handler can include it in the logout request.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: set firmware_version_bytes to show 4.1.6.2 as Dante Version in DC

firmware_version_bytes controls the board_info packet (offset 0x00..0x04)
which is what Dante Controller displays as 'Dante Version'.
product_version_bytes controls the product_info packet — a separate field.

Both are now overridden to 4.1.6.2 so DC shows the correct version
in all version-related fields.

Bumps inferno dep to 8cf07f0 which adds firmware_version_bytes override
to DeviceInfo (same pattern as product_version_bytes at 7d7bef8).

commit.message:
feat: add firmware_version_bytes override to DeviceInfo

Allows callers to set the Dante Version shown in Dante Controller
independently of the inferno_aoip crate version.
Set DeviceInfo::firmware_version_bytes = Some([major, minor, H(patch), L(patch)])
to override; None falls back to CARGO_PKG_VERSION_*.

This is the board_info packet field at offset 0x00..0x04 that DC
displays as 'Dante Version' — separate from product_version_bytes
which controls the 'Product Version' field.

Add chunked date-range retry for large repos (502 fallback) (#73)

* Add chunked date-range retry for large repos (502 fallback)

When the full 90-day merged-PR query returns persistent HTTP 502 (as
happens with high-volume repos like dotnet/dotnet-api-docs), the second
pass now splits the date range into 30-day chunks and queries each
independently.

Changes:
- Add optional EndDate parameter to Invoke-RepoMaintainerScan to
  support merged:A..B date-range queries
- Add Invoke-ChunkedRepoScan function that computes non-overlapping
  chunks, scans each, and merges results (MergerCounts summed,
  Activity paths/labels concatenated)
- Replace second-pass same-query retry with chunked scan
- Add ChunkDays validation (1-365)
- Add 11 Pester tests for chunk computation, result merging, partial
  failure handling, and EndDate parameter passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

so upgraded installs crash at query time instead of self-healing.

Teach migrateDb() to add the missing columns in place and add a regression
test that reproduces the old schema and verifies the vector queries no
longer throw.

  Xray-linux-64.zip 和 Xray-linux-arm64-v8a.zip
- curl 缺少 --fail 参数，404 页面被当作正常下载

修复内容：
- check_arch() 改为返回 64 / arm64-v8a 匹配 release 命名
- 所有 curl 下载改用 -fSL，HTTP 错误立即失败
- 子 shell 拆分为独立语句，set -e 可正确捕获失败

- update_geodata 末尾增加文件存在性验证

docs: add ADR-0001 system architecture (accepted)

ADR-0001 defines the foundational architecture for Naked
Time:

- Background daemon (timed) with user-scoped lifecycle
  via launchd, systemd, and Windows Task Scheduler
- Unix domain sockets for IPC on all platforms including
  Windows 11
- gRPC as the primary API protocol with protobuf contracts
- SQLite in WAL mode for local data storage
- TOML configuration
- Plugin architecture with child process supervision and
  per-plugin UDS
- Python AI agent via LangChain Deep Agents SDK
- Separate MCP server binary (time-mcp)
- Go as the implementation language for all core components
  (timed, time-cli, time-mcp)
- Cross-compilation targeting 9 platforms via GOOS/GOARCH
